items.title: 'Living in a warming world requires practical solutions that save lives, don't harm our environment'
items.description: As Europe confronts increasingly frequent and intense heatwaves, the conversation around climate adaptation is shifting from environmental policy to public health, education, and social infrastructure. Haxie Meyers-Belkin is pleased to welcome Caradee Wright, Chief Specialist Scientist at the South African Medical Research Council leading the Climate and Health Research Programme. She argues that schools have become one of the clearest frontlines of climate change. Her analysis moves beyond the immediate debate over air conditioning to present a more systemic vision of heat resilience, combining architecture, behavioural adaptation, public awareness, and educational policy.Rather than framing extreme heat as a temporary disruption, Wright treats it as a defining condition of the coming decades that demands structural change. She highlights children's unique physiological vulnerability, questions whether historic school buildings remain fit for a warming climate, and argues that protecting education requires redesigning both physical spaces and institutional routines.

items.title: Afghanistan's Taliban government imposes smartphone ban on government officials
items.description: Rights advocates said smartphones remain ⁠one of the few tools Afghans use to access education, document abuses, communicate privately and reach uncensored information

items.title: US strikes Iran after Trump says ceasefire was violated
Hezbollah will not support Israel-Lebanon framework deal
items.description: Al Jazeera brings you the latest from Beirut as the US announces a framework agreement between Israel and Lebanon.

items.title: Minimum wage has lost 40% of its purchasing power under Milei
items.description: Basic income for registered workers has plunged against November 2023 to levels even inferior to the 2001 crisis, according to a report by UBA Buenos Aires University’s Economics Faculty. Economic growth produces winners but they are not a majority. Leer más

items.title: INDEC says Argentina's unemployment rate stood at 7.8% in first quarter
items.description: According to INDEC, 1.1 million people are out of work and searching for jobs; Those in employment represent 44.8% of the population, equivalent to 13.5 million people. Leer más

items.title: Four men held over child marriage in Sierra Leone appear in landmark court case
items.description: It relates to a 17-year-old girl - four men including her father and husband face years in jail if convicted.

items.title: How much disaster aid will the US provide to Venezuela?
items.description: Powerful back-to-back earthquakes leave behind destruction.

items.title: Why Taylor Swift's rumored wedding has fans watching New York
items.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are rumored to celebrate at Madison Square Garden on July 3, with speculation ranging from a wedding reception to surprise musical performances.

items.title: Lalo Schifrin’s final symphony, Rod Schejtman named Argentine cultural ambassador
items.description: Rod Schejtman, the composer Lalo Schifrin designated as his artistic heir, now carries Argentina’s national brand to the world’s concert halls. Leer más

items.title: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
items.description: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

items.title: With or without Messi? Argentina's big question against Jordan
items.description: Saturday's rotated side could still include players eager to maintain rhythm and those seeking it. Julián Álvarez is expected to start, with changes to the defence and midfield also likely. Leer más

items.title: Hundreds arrested in Nairobi on anniversary of 2024 Gen Z protests
items.description: More than 300 people were arrested during protests in Nairobi on Thursday, police say, as Kenya marked two years since a landmark Gen Z uprising.

items.title: Swatch seeks $170 million in damages from Samsung over trademark infringement
Why Germans don't have air conditioning
items.description: Unlike the US or parts of Asia, many homes in Germany and northern Europe aren't equipped to deal with extreme heat. But as the world gets hotter, trends are changing.

items.title: Olive industry 'buzzing' as diets fuel demand, farmers diversify
items.description: Consumer interest in olive oil has boomed at the same time as growing the produce has flourished, leading to excitement in the industry.

items.title: Frozen feasts and cool pools help Rome zoo animals survive heatwave
items.description: As a severe heatwave grips Italy, Rome's Bioparco is helping its animals stay cool with frozen treats, chilled pools and climate-controlled shelters while the capital remains under a red heat alert.

items.title: Middle East War:  US strikes Iran after Trump says ceasefire was violated
items.description: The US struck Iran on Friday to respond to a drone attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, a provocation that US President Donald Trump said violated the ceasefire. The British military said Thursday that a vessel was hit by a projectile off Oman. This comes at a fragile time for the US and Iranian negotiations over interim peace deal. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Sudan: Fears of atrocities as RSF surrounds key city
items.description: As Sudan's paramilitary Rapid Support Forces mass around the strategic city of el-Obeid, fears are growing of atrocities similar to those seen in Darfur's el-Fasher. Could sanctions still prevent bloodshed?

items.title: Death toll from double earthquake strike in Venezuela rises to 188
items.description: Venezuela mounts desperate search for survivors after twin quakes kill at least 188 and injure hundreds; Offers of rescue support, aid and condolences pour in. Leer más

items.title: Falling energy prices won't defuse rising risk of civil unrest in emerging markets
US sanctions Rwanda gold refinery accused of smuggling DR Congo's minerals
items.description: The US alleges Gasabo Gold Refinery smuggled at least 60kg of gold in early 2026.

items.title: Magdeburg Christmas market attacker sentenced to life in prison
items.description: A Magdeburg court has sentenced a 51-year-old to life over his 2024 attack on a Christmas market in the city. The trained psychiatrist drove an SUV through a barricade and into a crowd, killing six and injuring hundreds.

items.title: Lebanon: thousands mark Ashoura in Nabatieh amid rubble left by Israeli strikes
items.description: Thousands of Shiite Muslims marked Ashoura in Nabatieh on 26 June, holding processions through neighbourhoods devastated by recent Israeli airstrikes in a powerful display of resilience.

items.title: Montenegro arrests Iran-linked hacker wanted by US
items.description: The FBI was helping the Montenegrin police detain an Iranian-Turkish national whose cyberattacks on US infrastructure allegedly caused billions in damages.

items.title: Anthropic accuses Chinese rival Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities
items.description: The firm alleged that Alibaba used fraudulent accounts to access data from its Claude AI model.

items.title: Oil price falls back to pre-Iran war levels
items.description: Signs that traffic through the key Strait of Hormuz shipping route is gradually resuming has helped to push the oil price down.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: How Big Tech is changing journalism
items.description: Technology companies are shaping how news is presented and digested, leaving media outlets little option but to comply. At DW's Global Media Forum in Bonn, participants discussed how to resist and adapt.

items.title: Middlemen offer Iranian oil to Indian refiners after U.S. waiver, sources say
items.description: NIOC is telling Indian buyers that Iranian crude would be $3 to $4 a barrel cheaper ‌than similar regional grades on a landed basis, they said

items.title: Milei settles Argentina's US$15-million UN debt
items.description: Milei administration pays off Argentina’s outstanding US$15.6-million debt with the United Nations as campaigning for Rafael Grossi's secretary-general candidacy enters key stretch. Leer más

items.title: Milei shuns Wall Street to raise dollars at rate lower than 7%
items.description: Luis Caputo stuck to a strategy that many investors initially saw as too risky: secure dollars through cheaper alternative channels, buy time and wait for spreads to come down. Leer más

items.title: Plane crashes into Beijing's tallest building; damage reported
items.description: The cause of the damage could not be independently verified, and authorities did not immediately issue a statement on the incident.

items.title: UN rights chief calls for probe into migrant deaths in US detention centres
items.description: Deaths of immigrants held in US detention centres have surged during Donald Trump's second term.

items.title: Senior Ukrainian intelligence official jailed for life for spying for Russia
items.description: Col Dmytro Kozyura agreed to share state secrets and systematically disclosed classified information, prosecutors say.

items.title: In some Mexican regions, endemic violence dampens World Cup excitement
items.description: Much of Mexico has been celebrating the World Cup with joyful outdoor gatherings, filling streets, plazas and fan zones in the country's three host cities — Mexico City, Guadalajara and Monterrey — since the tournament kickoff on June 11. But in parts of the country plagued by cartel violence, the event is experienced differently: with fear.

items.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll rises to around 589
items.description: The number of dead and injured is expected to rise with thousands reported missing

items.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants
items.description: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.

items.title: Watch: Eight arrested over Ram temple donations scandal | Above the Fold | 26.06.2026
items.description: From arrests over the alleged embezzlement of Ram temple donations and a standoff between Nihang Sikhs and the police in Uttarakhand, to the release of the names of servicemen who died during Operation Sindoor, the rising death toll from Venezuela’s earthquakes, revised fuel prices, higher Apple product prices, FIFA highlights, and more.

items.title: U.S. Supreme Court’s ruling to end protections for Haitian, Syrian immigrants could have broader impact
items.description: The Supreme Court ’s decision allowing President Donald Trump’s administration to end temporary legal protections for Haitian and Syrian immigrants may affect people from many other countries

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: Philippines bans video game played by alleged high school shooter
items.description: The rare school shooting that left three students killed and 20 others injured has shocked the country.
